title: Integration
summary: Free Cambridge 9709 Pure Maths 1 integration — reverse power rule, definite integrals as signed area, and Cambridge mark-scheme habits.

simpleExplanation.title: Area under the curve
simpleExplanation.summary: Integration is the reverse of differentiation. On Paper 1 you find antiderivatives, evaluate definite integrals as signed area, and always show substitution of limits.
steps: Start from a gradient function f(x) and find F(x) such that F′(x) = f(x).
A definite integral ∫ₐᵇ f(x) dx equals the signed area between the curve and the x-axis.
Evaluate F(b) − F(a) — show each limit substituted for method marks.
For indefinite integrals include + C; constants cancel when you use definite limits.

sections.content: **Integration on Paper 1** is the inverse of differentiation. You will reverse the power rule, interpret definite integrals as **signed area**, and evaluate limits cleanly — examiners award marks for showing **F(b) − F(a)** with substitution, not just a final number.
If dy/dx = x^n then y = x^(n+1)/(n+1) + C (n ≠ −1). For definite integrals: ∫ₐᵇ x^n dx = [x^(n+1)/(n+1)]ₐᵇ.

items: Increase the power by 1, divide by the new power
Definite integral = area above the axis minus area below
Always write square brackets before substituting limits
Include + C only for indefinite integrals

sections.content: Sketch the region when a question asks for **area bounded by curves**. If part of the graph lies below the x-axis, that portion contributes **negative** area — Cambridge often tests this with a cubic crossing the axis.

sections.question: Find ∫₁³ (2x − 1) dx.
sections.solution:
∫ (2x − 1) dx = x² − x + C.

Definite value: [x² − x]₁³ = (9 − 3) − (1 − 1) = 6.

sections.content: Differentiate your answer in one line to sanity-check. For area questions, state whether regions below the axis are subtracted — a quick sketch prevents sign errors.

items.label: B1 M1 A1 mark scheme guide

steps.caption: Integration reverses differentiation on P1 — find F(x) such that F′(x) = f(x).
steps.embedHint: Watch the antiderivative curve build from the gradient function.

steps.caption: Definite integral ∫ₐᵇ f(x) dx equals area under the curve (with sign).
steps.embedHint: Shade the region — negative regions subtract from total area.

steps.caption: Evaluate F(b) − F(a) — always show substitution of limits for A marks.
steps.embedHint: Move limits and read the accumulated area value.

steps.caption: Include + C for indefinite integrals; constants disappear in definite limits.
steps.embedHint: Compare indefinite and definite integral values on the same graph.
